extraction_notes: Most papers obtained from PMC OA full-text. Wang 2020 Nature, Waters 2012 Development, Dun 2023 PCP, and Nahas 2024 NPH and Braun 2012 PP were partially extracted (abstract + journal landing page). Two papers in the batch list (Brewer 2015 'SL Inhibition...PAT' Plant Physiol, Brewer 2009 'SL Acts Downstream of Auxin') overlap topically — both fully read. Wang 2020 Nature edges focus on transcriptional targets identified by RNA-seq + ChIP-seq.

edges.mechanism: Auxin promotes expression of SL biosynthesis gene CCD8/MAX4 in an AXR1-dependent manner; decapitation reduces auxin and reduces CCD8/MAX4 transcript

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin regulation of the expression of the SMS synthesis gene CCD8 was demonstrated to be AXR1 dependent.
evidence.claim: Auxin upregulates CCD8/MAX4 (and other SL biosynthesis genes) via AXR1-dependent signaling

edges.mechanism: Auxin promotes CCD7/MAX3 transcription; decapitation reduces IAA and reduces CCD7/MAX3 transcript

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Decapitation resulted in reduced IAA levels and decreased expression of SMS synthesis genes.
evidence.claim: Auxin upregulates CCD7/MAX3 (decapitation reduces IAA and CCD7 transcript)

edges.mechanism: Auxin promotes SL biosynthesis (via upregulation of MAX3/MAX4 etc.); the primary repressor of bud outgrowth is auxin-dependent SL production

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: the primary repressor of bud outgrowth appears to be the auxin-dependent production of strigolactones.
evidence.claim: Auxin promotes strigolactone biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Exogenous SL (GR24) directly inhibits axillary bud outgrowth even when auxin is depleted; thus SL acts downstream of auxin to inhibit branching

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: exogenously applied strigolactone inhibited bud outgrowth in pea even when auxin was depleted after decapitation.
evidence.claim: Strigolactone inhibits axillary bud outgrowth

edges.mechanism: MAX2/RMS4 F-box protein is required for strigolactone signal transduction (downstream perception of SL inhibits bud release)

evidence.title: Strigolactone Acts Downstream of Auxin to Regulate Bud Outgrowth in Pea and Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Ferguson BJ, Rameau C, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX2/RMS4 F-box protein is required for strigolactone inhibition of bud release.
evidence.claim: MAX2 is required for SL-mediated inhibition of bud outgrowth (SL signaling pathway)

edges.mechanism: Auxin promotes PIN polarization on basal plasma membrane; this auxin-feedback on PIN drives canalization (auxin transport)

evidence.title: Strigolactones inhibit auxin feedback on PIN-dependent auxin transport canalization
evidence.authors: Mazur E, Kulik I, Hajny J, Friml J
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin-mediated PIN2 lateralization...was consistently attenuated by induction of SL biosynthesis.
evidence.claim: Auxin polarizes PIN to promote canalization (and SL antagonizes this auxin feedback)

edges.mechanism: SL signaling (D14/MAX2-dependent) inhibits auxin-feedback on PIN polarity, reducing PIN polarization and PIN-dependent auxin canalization; effect requires ubiquitination/proteasome

evidence.title: Strigolactones inhibit auxin feedback on PIN-dependent auxin transport canalization
evidence.authors: Mazur E, Kulik I, Hajny J, Friml J
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14 receptor- and MAX2 F-box-mediated SL signaling inhibits the formation of auxin-conducting channels after wounding or from artificial auxin sources.
evidence.claim: SL signaling (D14/MAX2-dependent) inhibits PIN polarization and PIN-mediated auxin canalization

edges.mechanism: PIN1 protein at basal plasma membrane mediates polar auxin efflux; SMXL7 increases PIN1-GFP at basal plasma membrane and increases auxin transport through stem segments

evidence.title: SMAX1-LIKE7 Signals from the Nucleus to Regulate Shoot Development in Arabidopsis via Partially EAR Motif-Independent Mechanisms
evidence.authors: Liang Y, Ward S, Li P, Bennett T, Leyser O
evidence.evidence_sentence: auxin transport through stem segments was increased in SMXL7d53 line.
evidence.claim: PIN1 polarization on basal plasma membrane drives polar auxin transport through stem

edges.mechanism: SL (via D14-MAX2-SMXL678 complex) leads to transcriptional activation of BRC1, directly regulating shoot branching

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of strigolactone signalling in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wang L, Wang B, Yu H, Guo H, Lin T, Kou L, Wang A, Shao N, Ma H, Xiong G, Li X, Yang J, Chu J, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: these plant hormones regulate shoot branching, leaf shape and anthocyanin accumulation mainly through transcriptional activation of the BRANCHED 1, TCP DOMAIN PROTEIN 1 and PRODUCTION OF ANTHOCYANIN PIGMENT 1 genes.
evidence.claim: SL transcriptionally activates BRC1, TCP1, and PAP1

edges.mechanism: SL transcriptionally activates TCP DOMAIN PROTEIN 1 via D14-MAX2-SMXL678 pathway

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of strigolactone signalling in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wang L, Wang B, Yu H, Guo H, Lin T, Kou L, Wang A, Shao N, Ma H, Xiong G, Li X, Yang J, Chu J, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: these plant hormones regulate shoot branching, leaf shape and anthocyanin accumulation mainly through transcriptional activation of the BRANCHED 1, TCP DOMAIN PROTEIN 1 and PRODUCTION OF ANTHOCYANIN PIGMENT 1 genes.
evidence.claim: SL activates TCP1 transcription

edges.mechanism: SMXL6 directly binds promoters of SMXL6/7/8 to repress their transcription, forming an autoregulatory negative feedback loop that maintains SL signaling homeostasis

evidence.title: Transcriptional regulation of strigolactone signalling in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wang L, Wang B, Yu H, Guo H, Lin T, Kou L, Wang A, Shao N, Ma H, Xiong G, Li X, Yang J, Chu J, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL6 targets 729 genes in the Arabidopsis genome and represses the transcription of SMXL6, SMXL7 and SMXL8 by binding directly to their promoters.
evidence.claim: SMXL6 acts as autoregulated transcription factor that represses SMXL6/7/8 transcription

edges.mechanism: MAX2 (F-box of SCF complex) polyubiquitinates SMXL6/7/8 upon SL perception; SMXL6/7/8 are then degraded by 26S proteasome

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX2 is responsible for the polyubiquitination of...target proteins...degraded by 26S proteasome.
evidence.claim: MAX2 mediates polyubiquitination and proteasomal degradation of SMXL6/7/8

edges.mechanism: D14 receptor binds SL, induces conformational change, then targets SMXL6/7/8 for ubiquitination by SCF-MAX2 complex (D14-MAX2-SMXL678)

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: ubiquitination of certain target proteins from...SMXL family, which are consequently degraded.
evidence.claim: D14 mediates SL-induced degradation of SMXL6/7/8 via SCF-MAX2 ubiquitin ligase

edges.mechanism: SMXL6 represses transcription factors controlling BRC1 expression; SMXL678 degradation by SL relieves this repression so BRC1 increases under SL

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMXL6 can repress transcription factors that control expression of BRANCHED 1 (BRC1).
evidence.claim: SMXL6 represses BRC1 expression (indirectly via TF cascade)

edges.mechanism: SMXL678 represses BRC1 and other branching-inhibitory factors; therefore high SMXL678 promotes branching, and SL-induced SMXL678 degradation inhibits branching

evidence.title: SMAX1-LIKE7 Signals from the Nucleus to Regulate Shoot Development in Arabidopsis via Partially EAR Motif-Independent Mechanisms
evidence.authors: Liang Y, Ward S, Li P, Bennett T, Leyser O
evidence.evidence_sentence: loss of SMXL7 function...is sufficient to suppress completely the SL-related shoot branching phenotype.
evidence.claim: SMXL678 promotes shoot branching; loss of SMXL678 suppresses branching

edges.mechanism: SMXL6/7/8 contains EAR motif that recruits TOPLESS/TPR corepressors to repress target gene expression

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: EAR motif...enable interaction with...CTLH domain...transcriptional corepressors...TOPLESS.
evidence.claim: SMXL678 EAR motif recruits TOPLESS/TPR corepressors

edges.mechanism: BRC1 (TCP transcription factor) is required for branching inhibition; brc1 mutant shows increased branching and is unresponsive to GR24

evidence.title: The Pea TCP Transcription Factor PsBRC1 Acts Downstream of Strigolactones to Control Shoot Branching
evidence.authors: Braun N, de Saint Germain A, Pillot JP, Boutet-Mercey S, Dalmais M, Antoniadi I, Li X, Maia-Grondard A, Le Signor C, Bouteiller N, Luo D, Bendahmane A, Turnbull C, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: The pea Psbrc1 mutant displays an increased shoot-branching phenotype, is able to synthesize strigolactone, and does not respond to strigolactone application.
evidence.claim: BRC1 inhibits shoot branching downstream of SL signaling

edges.mechanism: GR24 application directly upregulates PsBRC1 transcript in pea axillary buds

evidence.title: The Pea TCP Transcription Factor PsBRC1 Acts Downstream of Strigolactones to Control Shoot Branching
evidence.authors: Braun N, de Saint Germain A, Pillot JP, Boutet-Mercey S, Dalmais M, Antoniadi I, Li X, Maia-Grondard A, Le Signor C, Bouteiller N, Luo D, Bendahmane A, Turnbull C, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: transcriptionally up-regulated by direct application of the synthetic SL GR24.
evidence.claim: SL (GR24) directly upregulates BRC1 transcription in axillary buds

edges.mechanism: Cytokinin (6-BAP) downregulates BRC1 transcript, releasing branching inhibition

evidence.title: The Pea TCP Transcription Factor PsBRC1 Acts Downstream of Strigolactones to Control Shoot Branching
evidence.authors: Braun N, de Saint Germain A, Pillot JP, Boutet-Mercey S, Dalmais M, Antoniadi I, Li X, Maia-Grondard A, Le Signor C, Bouteiller N, Luo D, Bendahmane A, Turnbull C, Rameau C
evidence.evidence_sentence: down-regulated by the cytokinin (CK) 6-benzylaminopurine.
evidence.claim: Cytokinin represses BRC1 transcription

edges.mechanism: SL binds D14 (alpha/beta hydrolase), triggering hydrolysis and a covalent His247-D-ring intermediate; this conformational change recruits MAX2 to initiate signaling

evidence.title: DWARF14 is a non-canonical hormone receptor for strigolactone (de Saint Germain analog: An histidine covalent receptor and butenolide complex mediates strigolactone perception)
evidence.authors: de Saint Germain A, Clave G, Badet-Denisot MA, Pillot JP, Corber B, Cardoso C, Rameau C, Boyer FD
evidence.evidence_sentence: RMS3 acts as a single-turnover enzyme that explains its apparent low enzymatic rate.
evidence.claim: SL binds D14 and triggers hydrolysis with covalent intermediate formation; D14 is the SL receptor

edges.mechanism: Upon SL binding and hydrolysis, D14 undergoes open-to-closed conformational change and recruits the F-box protein MAX2/D3 of the SCF E3 ligase complex

evidence.title: DWARF14 is a non-canonical hormone receptor for strigolactone
evidence.authors: Yao R, Ming Z, Yan L, et al. (paper at 10.1038/nature19073 is Yao 2016; de Saint Germain published 10.1038/nchembio.2147 in 2016)
evidence.evidence_sentence: AtD14 undergoes an open-to-closed state transition to trigger strigolactone signalling, demonstrating that AtD14 generates and senses the active form of strigolactone.
evidence.claim: D14 conformational change recruits MAX2 to form active signaling complex

edges.mechanism: D14 hydrolyzes SL producing D-OH that remains in catalytic pocket; the altered D14 surface enables interaction with DELLA repressor SLR1, providing GA-SL crosstalk

evidence.title: Molecular mechanism of strigolactone perception by DWARF14
evidence.authors: Zhao LH, Zhou XE, Wu ZS, Yi W, Xu Y, Li S, Xu TH, Liu Y, Chen RZ, Kovach A, Kang Y, Hou L, He Y, Xie C, Song W, Zhong D, Xu Y, Wang Y, Li J, Zhang C, Melcher K, Xu HE
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14 functions as a cleavage enzyme of SLs, and the cleavage reaction induces the interaction with SLR1.
evidence.claim: SL-induced D14 conformational change enables interaction with DELLA (SLR1), connecting SL and GA pathways

edges.mechanism: GR24/SL induces rapid degradation of D53 protein via D14 receptor and SCF(D3/MAX2) E3 ligase polyubiquitination, mediated by 26S proteasome

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.authors: Jiang L, Liu X, Xiong G, Liu H, Chen F, Wang L, Meng X, Liu G, Yu H, Yuan Y, Yi W, Zhao L, Ma H, He Y, Wu Z, Melcher K, Qian Q, Xu HE, Wang Y, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: GR24 cause D53 degradation via the proteasome in a manner that requires D14 and the SCFD3.
evidence.claim: SL induces D53 degradation via D14 + SCF(D3) ubiquitin ligase

edges.mechanism: D53 (rice ortholog of SMXL6/7/8) represses SL signaling; gain-of-function dominant d53 mutant is SL-insensitive and shows excessive tillering/branching

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.authors: Jiang L, Liu X, Xiong G, Liu H, Chen F, Wang L, Meng X, Liu G, Yu H, Yuan Y, Yi W, Zhao L, Ma H, He Y, Wu Z, Melcher K, Qian Q, Xu HE, Wang Y, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 functions as a repressor of SL signalling.
evidence.claim: D53 represses SL signaling, promoting tillering/branching; rice ortholog of SMXL6/7/8

edges.mechanism: D53 contains EAR-like motifs that recruit TOPLESS-RELATED PROTEIN (TPR) corepressors to repress downstream gene expression

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.authors: Jiang L, Liu X, Xiong G, Liu H, Chen F, Wang L, Meng X, Liu G, Yu H, Yuan Y, Yi W, Zhao L, Ma H, He Y, Wu Z, Melcher K, Qian Q, Xu HE, Wang Y, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: D53 can interact with transcriptional co-repressors known as TOPLESS-RELATED PROTEINS.
evidence.claim: D53 (and homologs SMXL678) recruit TPR corepressors to repress transcription

edges.mechanism: Loss of D53 causes upregulation of D10 (rice CCD8 ortholog), suggesting D53 represses SL biosynthesis gene transcription as feedback regulation

evidence.title: D14-SCFD3-dependent degradation of D53 regulates strigolactone signaling
evidence.authors: Zhou F, Lin Q, Zhu L, Ren Y, Zhou K, Shabek N, Wu F, Mao H, Dong W, Gan L, Ma W, Gao H, Chen J, Yang C, Wang D, Tan J, Zhang X, Guo X, Wang J, Jiang L, Liu X, Chen W, Chu J, Yan C, Ueno K, Ito S, Asami T, Cheng Z, Wang J, Lei C, Zhai H, Wu C, Wang H, Zheng N, Wan J
evidence.evidence_sentence: expression of D10...was similarly up-regulated in d53.
evidence.claim: D53 represses D10/CCD8 transcription; loss of D53 leads to elevated D10 (negative feedback on SL biosynthesis)

edges.mechanism: GR24/SL induces D14-D53 complex formation; D14 catalytic triad mutation weakens D53-D14 binding

evidence.title: DWARF 53 acts as a repressor of strigolactone signalling in rice
evidence.authors: Jiang L, Liu X, Xiong G, Liu H, Chen F, Wang L, Meng X, Liu G, Yu H, Yuan Y, Yi W, Zhao L, Ma H, He Y, Wu Z, Melcher K, Qian Q, Xu HE, Wang Y, Li J
evidence.evidence_sentence: mutation at any site of the [catalytic] triad could...weaken the interaction between D53 and D14.
evidence.claim: SL-bound D14 directly interacts with D53 (and SMXL678) to recruit them to SCF-MAX2 ubiquitin ligase

edges.mechanism: GR24 inhibits bud outgrowth in NPA-treated shoots independently of polar auxin transport — confirming a direct SL effect not mediated by auxin transport changes

evidence.title: Strigolactone Inhibition of Branching Independent of Polar Auxin Transport
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Gui R, Mason MG, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: strigolactones can inhibit bud outgrowth in NPA-treated shoots...despite...diminished auxin.
evidence.claim: SL inhibits axillary bud outgrowth via a mechanism independent of polar auxin transport (likely via BRC1)

edges.mechanism: KAI2 (paralog of D14) is required for karrikin signaling and acts via MAX2; MAX2 is shared between KAI2 and D14 pathways

evidence.title: Specialisation within the DWARF14 protein family confers distinct responses to karrikins and strigolactones in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Waters MT, Nelson DC, Scaffidi A, Flematti GR, Sun YK, Dixon KW, Smith SM
evidence.evidence_sentence: AtD14 and KAI2 define a class of proteins that permit the separate regulation of karrikin and strigolactone signalling by MAX2.
evidence.claim: KAI2 and D14 both signal through shared MAX2 F-box but mediate distinct karrikin/SL responses

edges.mechanism: KAI2-MAX2 signaling pathway targets SMAX1/SMXL2 for proteasomal degradation in karrikin response

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: KAI2-MAX2 signaling pathway has been proposed to only target SMAX1/SMXL2 for proteasomal degradation.
evidence.claim: KAI2 (with MAX2) mediates degradation of SMAX1/SMXL2 in karrikin signaling

edges.mechanism: GR24 stimulates D14-SMAX1 interaction; D14 mediates GR24-induced SMAX1 degradation in plants (weaker than D14-SMXL7 interaction); also activated by osmotic stress via SL biosynthesis induction

evidence.title: The strigolactone receptor D14 targets SMAX1 for degradation in response to GR24 treatment and osmotic stress
evidence.authors: Li Q, Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Khosla A, White ARF, Chang S, Cubas P, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: D14 mediates GR24-induced degradation of SMAX1 in plants.
evidence.claim: D14 receptor mediates GR24-induced SMAX1 degradation (cross-pathway with KAI2-SMAX1 axis)

edges.mechanism: Osmotic stress (mannitol/dehydration) induces SL biosynthesis genes, leading to SMAX1 degradation and stress tolerance

evidence.title: The strigolactone receptor D14 targets SMAX1 for degradation in response to GR24 treatment and osmotic stress
evidence.authors: Li Q, Sanchez Martin-Fontecha E, Khosla A, White ARF, Chang S, Cubas P, Nelson DC
evidence.evidence_sentence: SL biosynthesis genes are induced by dehydration or mild drought.
evidence.claim: Osmotic stress induces SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Strigolactone deficiency enhances auxin levels and/or transport as part of feedback regulation; SL-deficient mutants (max3/max4) have increased shoot auxin transport capacity

evidence.title: Strigolactone Inhibition of Branching Independent of Polar Auxin Transport
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Gui R, Mason MG, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: strigolactone depletion enhances auxin levels and/or transport as part of feedback mechanism.
evidence.claim: SL negatively regulates auxin levels/transport (feedback loop)

edges.mechanism: SLs regulate cytokinin metabolism (likely via promoting CKX or repressing IPT) — review-level synthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs regulate auxin transport, cytokinin metabolism, and ABA accumulation.
evidence.claim: SL regulates cytokinin metabolism (typically reducing CK levels)

edges.mechanism: SL signaling regulates ABA accumulation (review synthesis); plays role in drought/stress responses

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: SLs regulate auxin transport, cytokinin metabolism, and ABA accumulation.
evidence.claim: SL regulates ABA accumulation

edges.mechanism: MAX1 (CYP711A1) catalyzes carlactone-to-carlactonoic acid conversion as part of core SL biosynthesis pathway

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Key enzymes: D27, CCD7, CCD8 (plastid-localized core pathway); MAX1/CYP711A1; LBO (2OGDD enzyme); CLAMT.
evidence.claim: MAX1 (CYP711A1) is required for SL biosynthesis (carlactonoic acid step)

edges.mechanism: CCD7/MAX3 cleaves 9-cis-beta-carotene as initial step toward SL biosynthesis (plastid)

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: The SL biosynthesis pathway...is conserved for initial steps to carlactone (CL), named the core pathway.
evidence.claim: CCD7/MAX3 is required for SL biosynthesis (9-cis-beta-carotene cleavage)

edges.mechanism: CCD8/MAX4 catalyzes second cleavage step producing carlactone in plastid SL core biosynthesis pathway

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Key enzymes: D27, CCD7, CCD8 (plastid-localized core pathway).
evidence.claim: CCD8/MAX4 is required for SL biosynthesis (carlactone production)

edges.mechanism: D27 isomerase converts all-trans-beta-carotene to 9-cis-beta-carotene; first step of SL core biosynthesis

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Key enzymes: D27, CCD7, CCD8 (plastid-localized core pathway).
evidence.claim: D27 isomerase is required for SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: LBO (LATERAL BRANCHING OXIDOREDUCTASE, 2OGDD enzyme) catalyzes a downstream SL biosynthesis step producing canonical SLs

evidence.title: Strigolactones and Shoot Branching: What Is the Real Hormone and How Does It Work?
evidence.authors: Dun EA, Brewer PB, Gillam EMJ, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: MAX1/CYP711A1; LBO (2OGDD enzyme); CLAMT.
evidence.claim: LBO is required for downstream SL biosynthesis

edges.mechanism: Auxin inhibits cytokinin levels (apical dominance via reduced node-local CK biosynthesis)

evidence.title: Strigolactone Inhibition of Branching Independent of Polar Auxin Transport
evidence.authors: Brewer PB, Dun EA, Gui R, Mason MG, Beveridge CA
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin...inhibits cytokinin levels.
evidence.claim: Auxin inhibits cytokinin biosynthesis/levels

edges.mechanism: Microarray of max2 mutant shows SMXL2 and SMXL8 transcripts significantly down-regulated, suggesting MAX2 promotes SMXL2/SMXL8 transcription (likely via SMXL6 derepression in max2)

evidence.title: Strigolactone Signaling Genes Showing Differential Expression Patterns in Arabidopsis max Mutants
evidence.authors: Kumar M, Kim I, Kim YK, Heo JB, Suh MC, Kim HU
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMAX-LIKE2 (SMXL2) and SMAX-LIKE8 (SMXL8) expressions were significantly down-regulated in the max2 mutant.
evidence.claim: MAX2 mutation reduces SMXL2 and SMXL8 transcript levels (transcriptional feedback)

edges.mechanism: ACC4 (ethylene biosynthesis enzyme) is upregulated when SL signaling is active (max mutants show altered ACC4); SL signaling cooperates with ACC4 to inhibit branching

evidence.title: Strigolactone Signaling Genes Showing Differential Expression Patterns in Arabidopsis max Mutants
evidence.authors: Kumar M, Kim I, Kim YK, Heo JB, Suh MC, Kim HU
evidence.evidence_sentence: Cooperation between strigolactone signaling and ACC4.
evidence.claim: SL signaling activates ACC4 (ethylene biosynthesis) for branching inhibition; SMXL678 represses ACC4 in absence of SL

edges.mechanism: MAX1 overexpression (high SL) blocks vascular tissue regeneration after wounding; max1/max4/max2 mutants regenerate vasculature faster than WT

evidence.title: Strigolactones inhibit auxin feedback on PIN-dependent auxin transport canalization
evidence.authors: Mazur E, Kulik I, Hajny J, Friml J
evidence.evidence_sentence: Dex-treated DEX>>MAX1 plants...there was no regeneration of vasculature...after Dex induction.
evidence.claim: SL blocks vasculature regeneration via inhibition of auxin canalization

edges.mechanism: Auxin transport network alterations affect duration of slow lag phase before rapid bud outgrowth — auxin transport out of bud must be canalized before commitment to rapid growth

evidence.title: The activation of Arabidopsis axillary buds involves a switch from slow to rapid committed outgrowth regulated by auxin and strigolactone
evidence.authors: Nahas Z, Ticchiarelli F, van Rongen M, Dillon J, Leyser O
evidence.evidence_sentence: buds establish canalised auxin transport out of the bud, after which they enter a rapid growth phase.
evidence.claim: Auxin transport canalization out of bud regulates the lag-to-rapid outgrowth switch

edges.mechanism: GR24 application affects both lag phase duration and maximum growth rate of branches (rapid phase)

evidence.title: The activation of Arabidopsis axillary buds involves a switch from slow to rapid committed outgrowth regulated by auxin and strigolactone
evidence.authors: Nahas Z, Ticchiarelli F, van Rongen M, Dillon J, Leyser O
evidence.evidence_sentence: strigolactone treatment and a neighboring bud additionally influenced the rapid growth phase, suggesting these factors may also influence the maximum growth rate of branches.
evidence.claim: SL inhibits both lag phase entry and maximum growth rate of activated buds

edges.mechanism: Atd14 mutant shows increased branching phenotype similar to max2; D14 is required for SL-mediated branching inhibition

evidence.title: Specialisation within the DWARF14 protein family confers distinct responses to karrikins and strigolactones in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Waters MT, Nelson DC, Scaffidi A, Flematti GR, Sun YK, Dixon KW, Smith SM
evidence.evidence_sentence: Atd14-1 has an increased branching phenotype similar to max2.
evidence.claim: AtD14 is required for SL-mediated inhibition of shoot branching

edges.mechanism: SMAX1/SMXL2 promote hypocotyl elongation in light-grown seedlings; loss of SMAX1/SMXL2 reduces hypocotyl length

evidence.title: Masks Start to Drop: Suppressor of MAX2 1-Like Proteins Reveal Their Many Faces
evidence.authors: Temmerman A, Guillory A, Bonhomme S, Goormachtig S, Struk S
evidence.evidence_sentence: SMAX1...together with SMXL2, in hypocotyl elongation.
evidence.claim: SMAX1/SMXL2 promote hypocotyl elongation

perturbations_extracted.evidence_sentence: repeated application of GR24...completely inhibited bud outgrowth.
ccd8 mutant + GR24 inhibited bud growth from outset.
axr1 mutant + GR24 47% reduction in branch number.

perturbations_extracted.test_descriptor: tir1 afb1 afb2 afb3 quadruple + GR24
perturbations_extracted.evidence_sentence: tir1 afb1 afb2 afb3 quadruple + GR24 inhibited shoot bud outgrowth.
brc1 mutant...was not reduced by GR24.
Branching was significantly suppressed in yuc1D compared with wild type.
yuc1D x max3 highly branched phenotype (max3-like).
Wild-type + NPA increased branching below treatment site.
GR24 + NPA caused ~50% inhibition of bud outgrowth despite NPA blocking transport.
max1-1/max4-1 vascular regeneration faster than WT, more abundant vasculature.
regeneration occurred faster...and the regenerated strands were more abundant in max2-3.

perturbations_extracted.test_descriptor: d14 htl double KO + GR24 (PIN endocytosis)
perturbations_extracted.evidence_sentence: d14-1 htl-3 less sensitivity to GR24 antagonism of NAA effect.
smxl678 max2 mutant...has a sprawling growth habit with increased branching.
loss of SMXL7 function...is sufficient to suppress completely the SL-related shoot branching.
The pea Psbrc1 mutant displays an increased shoot-branching phenotype.
Psbrc1 mutant does not respond to strigolactone application.

perturbations_extracted.phenotype: PsBRC1 transcript level in axillary bud

literature_gaps_observed: No paper in this batch directly demonstrated AUXIN -> MAX1 transcriptional regulation in Arabidopsis at promoter level (only general SMS biosynthesis genes in pea/Arabidopsis from Brewer 2009). MAX1 transcriptional regulation by auxin needs to be sourced from Hayward 2009 or Bennett 2006 (different batch).
No paper provided direct evidence for AUXIN -> LBO (LBO is too recent — Brewer 2016 LBO paper not in batch but LBO induction by auxin should be checked there).
AUXIN -> D27 transcriptional regulation: not directly shown in this batch. D27 is in the SL biosynthesis pathway but its auxin regulation is less characterized (likely in Liu 2009 D27 paper).
Cytokinin biosynthesis (IPT) regulation by SL: review-level mentions only (Dun 2023). Need primary papers (e.g., Dun 2012 PP) for direct evidence.
ABA-SL crosstalk direction is bidirectional in literature; this batch mainly shows SL -> ABA induction (osmotic stress context) but lacks tissue-specific bud-level evidence.

PsBRC1 induction by SL was shown in pea (Braun 2012); equivalent direct GR24 -> AtBRC1 transcriptional induction should exist in Wang 2020 ChIP-seq data but exact gene-level fold-changes not extractable from abstract.
BRC1 downstream targets (e.g., HB21, HB40, HB53, NCED3, TPS1) — none captured in this SL-focused batch; covered by the BRC1 batch (B3).
